results.possibleIdentity: Melvin Fischer was an electronics engineer and executive who served as the President of Boro Electronics Inc. during the late 1960s and early 1970s. His company, Boro Electronics, was headquartered at 86 Denton Avenue (and later 130 County Courthouse Road) in New Hyde Park, New York, with additional operations previously located in Woodside, Queens. The firm was involved in the design, manufacture, and distribution of electronic components and systems, including aerospace-electrical contracts and environmental research through its subsidiary, Environmental Pollution Research Corporation. Fischer is associated with the non-profit organization Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), which facilitated collaborations between the technical community and artists. His participation in E.A.T. likely involved providing technical expertise or resources from Boro Electronics for artistic projects during the organization's peak period of activity in the New York area.
